headline: Production tooling for AI agents leads the week as voice, file handling, and audio infrastructure rotate to the front.
body: The strongest rotation is toward the execution layer around agents: file handling and audio top the movers, and concrete tooling lands across both. Antigravity automates file system workflows by time boxes and tags [0], the TEN Framework builds interruptible real-time voice agents with VAD, turn detection, and collision detection [8], and a separate piece lays out five components an agent harness needs to reach production [9]. Gemini also surfaces through Canvas, which generates websites, no-code apps, dashboards, and audio podcasts from prompts [10]. On technique, human-in-the-loop and observability are breaking out, with newer entrants including posterior collapse, parameterization, and latent dynamics learning.

cited_items.title: Automating workflows and file organization with Antigravity
TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
5 things every AI agent harness needs
Gemini Canvas Tutorial: How to Use Google's Most Underrated AI Tool
